message:
Fix safety, correctness, and security issues across 15 source files

Signal handling (action/signal.rs):
- Add PID range validation (reject target_id > i32::MAX) to prevent
  undefined behavior from truncating u32 to i32 in libc::kill calls
- Add PID-reuse detection before SIGKILL escalation: read /proc/PID/stat
  starttime and compare against the original start_id to avoid killing a
  replacement process that reused the same PID during the SIGTERM grace
  period (TOCTOU mitigation)
- Add ids_match_starttime() helper that extracts the starttime component
  from composite start_id strings and compares with 150-tick tolerance
- Return identity-check failure (Ok(false)) when UID cannot be read,
  instead of silently falling through to Ok(true)

Config initialization (agent_init/config.rs):
- Replace three .expect() calls with .ok_or_else() returning the new
  ConfigError::InternalState variant, preventing panics if mcpServers,
  tools, or extensions have unexpected JSON types after initialization

Process data collection (collect/):
- proc_parsers.rs: Use safe indexing (content.get()) instead of direct
  slice indexing when parsing /proc/PID/stat after the comm field close
  paren, preventing panics on truncated stat content
- proc_parsers.rs: Add documentation clarifying FD inspection truncation
  semantics (info.count vs inspected_count when truncated=true)
- quick_scan.rs: Tighten ps output field count validation from 18+ to
  22+ fields (the parser accesses fields up to index 21) in both
  parse_ps_line and parse_ps_line_synthetic
- quick_scan.rs: Use saturating_mul for RSS/VSZ KB-to-bytes conversion
  to prevent overflow on processes with very large memory footprints
- tick_delta.rs: Return None when tick counter goes backwards (PID reuse
  or counter wrap) instead of producing nonsensical negative deltas
- tick_delta.rs: Clamp non-finite/non-positive n_eff_cores to thread
  count to prevent NaN propagation through all downstream CPU arithmetic

Decision engine (decision/):
- dependency_loss.rs: Remove dead from_impact_components() method that
  referenced ImpactComponents fields, reducing maintenance surface
- ope.rs: Fix IPS variance calculation — was dividing each weighted
  reward by n before subtracting the mean (also divided by n), which
  double-divided and produced incorrect variance estimates; now computes
  (wr - mean_wr).powi(2) directly

Inference (inference/):
- ctw.rs: Remove dead has_child() and child_mut() methods from CtwNode,
  reducing unused code surface
- kalman.rs: Fix log-likelihood computation — was adding
  TAU.ln() / 2.0 (= ln(2*pi)/2) but the term was already inside a
  -0.5 * (...) expression, producing -0.5 * ln(2*pi) / 2 instead of
  the correct -0.5 * ln(2*pi); now uses TAU.ln() without the halving

Main module (main.rs):
- Remove #[allow(dead_code)] annotations from GoalPlanOutput,
  parse_kill_loss, build_opt_candidates_for_goals,
  build_goal_plan_from_candidates, and goal_summary_json — these items
  are now used and the suppression was masking potential warnings
- Remove the unused goal_progress_json() function entirely

Plugin system (plugin/):
- manager.rs: Replace 8 .unwrap() calls on HashMap lookups with
  .ok_or_else() returning proper error types (EvidencePluginError or
  ActionPluginError), preventing panics if plugin state is unexpectedly
  missing
- manager.rs: Rewrite invoke_subprocess to use poll-based timeout with
  try_wait() + sleep loop instead of wait_with_output() followed by a
  post-hoc elapsed-time check; the old approach blocked indefinitely if
  the child never exited, making the timeout ineffective; now actively
  kills the child on timeout
- manager.rs: Read stdout/stderr from child pipes after process exit
  instead of relying on wait_with_output() which is no longer used
- manifest.rs: Reject plugin commands containing ".." path traversal
- manifest.rs: Canonicalize resolved command paths and verify they
  remain under the plugin directory, preventing symlink-based escapes
- manifest.rs: Validate that the resolved command path exists and is a
  regular file before accepting it

Supervision (supervision/):
- app_supervision.rs: Add shell_escape() function and apply it to all
  pm2, supervisorctl, and forever command targets, preventing shell
  injection via crafted process names or UIDs
- ipc.rs: Replace manual '@' prefix check + slice indexing with
  strip_prefix('@').is_some_and() for safer abstract socket path matching
- nohup.rs: Use content.get() with proper error propagation instead of
  direct slice indexing when parsing /proc/PID/stat, preventing panics
  on truncated stat content

Issue tracker (.beads/issues.jsonl):
- Update issue tracker entries with new and closed issues

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
